column1: #Text=The white wagtail is a small passerine bird in the family Motacillidae, which also includes pipits and longclaws.
#Text=The species breeds in the Palearctic zone in most of Europe and Asia and parts of North Africa; it also has a toehold in western Alaska as a scarce breeder.
#Text=It is resident in the mildest parts of its range, but otherwise migrates to Africa.
#Text=In total, there are between 9 and 11 subspecies of M. alba; in Ireland and Great Britain, the black-backed subspecies known as the pied wagtail predominates.
